model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Houston holds a decisive edge in defensive efficiency, ranking 4th nationally in adjusted defensive efficiency, which should disrupt Illinois's top-ranked offense and force turnovers in a high-stakes Sweet 16 matchup. While Illinois boasts the nation's best offensive rating and can score in bunches, Houston's elite defense, physicality, and experience in the Big 12 provide a more reliable path to victory in this neutral-site game. The underdog's path for Illinois relies on hot three-point shooting and winning the rebounding battle, but Houston's proven ability to control tempo and force mistakes makes them the pick to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Team Quality and Efficiency Comparison
reasoningStep.summary: Houston and Illinois are both elite teams, but Houston's defensive prowess gives them a slight edge in a matchup of offensive firepower versus defensive discipline.
reasoningStep.evidence: Houston ranks 5th in KenPom and Torvik with a top-5 defense (adjD 91.9), while Illinois ranks 7th with the nation's top offense (adjO 131.2) but a weaker defense (adjD 99.1).

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: s16-south-2
Current game label: South Sweet 16 2
Round: Sweet 16
Resolved teams: Illinois (illinois) vs Houston (houston)
Prior committed picks: 57

The 2026 NCAA Tournament gets rolling with the first round on Thursday, March 19, and Friday, March 20. What do the betting odds say about each March Madness game?
